target.name: ngouniensines
target.motivation: The 7-membered azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton is characteristic of a variety of indole alkaloids, including ngouniensines. The significance of this skeleton as an essential therapeutic pharmacophore has been highlighted by the development of tabernanthalog, a safer ibogaine analog. Existing synthetic methods for this skeleton mainly rely on inherent functional group reactivity, limiting structural diversity. This work aims to develop a C-H functionalization based approach to simplify retrosynthetic disconnections and provide a concise entry to ngouniensines.

success_criteria.free_text: Concise total synthesis of ngouniensines, demonstrating the synthetic potential of the developed C-H functionalization chemistry.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C-C bond forming the 7-membered azepino[4,5-b]indole core
key_disconnections.reasoning: The azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton can be constructed by direct C-H functionalization of 2-alkyl tryptamines, where the non-activated methylene at C2 participates in a 7-membered ring formation with aldehydes. This expands the scope of Pictet-Spengler type reactions.

key_disconnections.bond_description: C=C bond of the exocyclic conjugated alkene
key_disconnections.reasoning: The exocyclic conjugated alkene in ngouniensines can be introduced by a second C-H functionalization of the same carbon with formaldehyde.

retrosynthetic_strategy.rationale: The strategy aims to simplify retrosynthetic disconnections by avoiding the preinstallation of requisite functional groups, utilizing direct C-H functionalization for both the core skeleton formation and the introduction of the exocyclic alkene.

thought.background: The Pictet-Spengler type condensation is a classic reaction for indole-annulated 5-, 6-, and 8-membered heterocyclic rings. The 7-membered azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton is important but has fewer synthetic methods, often relying on pre-functionalized groups.
thought.gap: A direct C-H functionalization approach to form the 7-membered azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton from 2-alkyl tryptamines is lacking. Also, a C-H functionalization approach for the exocyclic alkene in ngouniensines would be beneficial.
thought.decision: To synthesize the azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton, we envisioned a direct C-H functionalization of 2-alkyl tryptamines (2) with aldehydes (3), where the non-activated methylene at C2 participates in a 7-membered ring formation. This would be a homologated Pictet-Spengler condensation. For ngouniensines, a second C-H functionalization with formaldehyde would introduce the exocyclic alkene.

evidence.text_span: Herein, we report an unprecedented approach for the synthesis of the azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton by the direct C–H functionalization of 2-alkyl tryptamines, and showcase the synthetic utility in the concise total synthesis of ngouniensines. ... Specically, as depicted in Fig. 1, we envisioned to construct the azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton by the direct C–H functionalization of 2-alkyl tryptamines 2, in which the non-activated methylene at C2 would participate in a 7- membered ring formation with aldehydes 3. ... The exocyclic conjugated alkene in ngouniensines could be introduced by a second C–H functionalization of the same carbon with formaldehyde (R1 = H).

thought.background: The proposed C-H functionalization for 7-membered ring formation. The mechanism is hypothesized to involve tautomerization of a spiroindolenine intermediate to an exocyclic enamine, followed by a rearrangement.
thought.gap: Identifying suitable acidic conditions to promote the reaction.
thought.decision: To test various Brønsted acids for the model reaction between 2a and 3a, expecting to find an effective catalyst for the proposed mechanism.

evidence.text_span: As depicted in the model reaction of 2a and 3a (Table 1), we envisioned that the tautomerization of spiroindolenine B (generated by the Pictet– Spengler reaction) to the exocyclic enamine C would set the basis for the C–H functionalization of the non-activated methyl group, and the 7-membered azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton would be subsequently formed via the intermediate D through either the concerted [3,3]-sigmatropic rearrangement or stepwise Mannich/retro Mannich pathway. Based on such a proposal, different Brønsted acids were screened with this model reaction.

thought.background: Screening Brønsted acids for the model reaction of 2a and 3a to form 4a.
thought.gap: Finding an acid that promotes the reaction effectively.
thought.decision: To use AcOH as the acid in chloroform, expecting some conversion based on its general acidity.

outcome.observation: The desired azepino[4,5-b]indole 4a was observed, but in only 11% yield.

evidence.text_span: Using AcOH as the acid in chloroform, we were pleased to observe the formation of the desired azepino[4,5-b] indole 4a, albeit in only 11% yield (Table 1, entry 1).

thought.background: AcOH gave low yield. Exploring other Brønsted acids.
thought.decision: To test camphorsulfonic acid (CSA) as a stronger Brønsted acid in chloroform, hoping for improved yield.

outcome.observation: Only a trace amount of product 4a was observed.

evidence.text_span: Camphorsulfonic acid (CSA, entry 2), p-toluene sulfonic acid (p-TsOH, entry 3) and diphenyl phosphate (entry 4) were not successful promotors for the formation of 4a.

thought.background: AcOH gave low yield, CSA gave trace. Exploring other Brønsted acids.
thought.decision: To test p-TsOH as a strong Brønsted acid in chloroform, hoping for improved yield.

outcome.observation: Only a trace amount of product 4a was observed.

evidence.text_span: Camphorsulfonic acid (CSA, entry 2), p-toluene sulfonic acid (p-TsOH, entry 3) and diphenyl phosphate (entry 4) were not successful promotors for the formation of 4a.

thought.background: AcOH gave low yield, CSA and p-TsOH gave trace. Exploring other Brønsted acids.
thought.decision: To test diphenyl phosphate as a Brønsted acid in chloroform, hoping for improved yield.

outcome.observation: Only a trace amount of product 4a was observed.

evidence.text_span: Camphorsulfonic acid (CSA, entry 2), p-toluene sulfonic acid (p-TsOH, entry 3) and diphenyl phosphate (entry 4) were not successful promotors for the formation of 4a.

thought.background: Previous acids (AcOH, CSA, p-TsOH, diphenyl phosphate) were ineffective or gave low yields.
thought.gap: Identifying an acid that provides a good yield for the model reaction.
thought.decision: To test trifluoroacetic acid (TFA) as a strong Brønsted acid in chloroform, expecting better catalytic activity.

outcome.observation: A promising result was obtained using TFA, delivering 4a in 52% yield.

evidence.text_span: A promising result was obtained using triuoroacetic acid (TFA), delivering 4a in 52% yield (entry 5).

thought.background: TFA showed promise with 52% yield. The amount of TFA might be critical.
thought.gap: Optimizing the equivalent of TFA for higher yield.
thought.decision: To reduce the equivalent of TFA to 1.0 equiv, to see if less acid is sufficient or if higher concentration is detrimental.

outcome.observation: Only a trace amount of product 4a was observed with 1.0 equivalent of TFA.

evidence.text_span: The equivalent of TFA was then found to be a key factor (entries 6–8). With 4.0 equivalent of TFA as the acid, 4a could be isolated in 85% yield (entry 7).

thought.background: 1.0 equivalent of TFA resulted in trace product, indicating that a higher concentration of TFA is beneficial.
thought.gap: Finding the optimal equivalent of TFA for maximum yield.
thought.decision: To increase the equivalent of TFA to 4.0 equiv, expecting a significant improvement in yield.

outcome.observation: With 4.0 equivalent of TFA, 4a was isolated in 85% yield.

evidence.text_span: The equivalent of TFA was then found to be a key factor (entries 6–8). With 4.0 equivalent of TFA as the acid, 4a could be isolated in 85% yield (entry 7).

thought.background: 4.0 equivalents of TFA gave 85% yield. Further increasing TFA might improve or decrease yield.
thought.gap: Determining if higher TFA equivalents are beneficial.
thought.decision: To increase the equivalent of TFA to 6.0 equiv, to see if the yield can be further optimized.

outcome.observation: Increasing TFA to 6.0 equivalents resulted in a decreased yield of 60%.

evidence.text_span: The equivalent of TFA was then found to be a key factor (entries 6–8). With 4.0 equivalent of TFA as the acid, 4a could be isolated in 85% yield (entry 7).

thought.background: Optimal TFA equivalent is 4.0. Now, solvent effects need to be explored.
thought.gap: Finding an optimal solvent for the reaction.
thought.decision: To test toluene as a solvent with 4.0 equivalents of TFA, to see if it performs better than chloroform.

outcome.observation: The reaction in toluene gave a lower yield of 56% compared to chloroform.

evidence.text_span: The reaction also proceeded in other solvents: similar yield using CH2Cl2, albeit with lower yield using toluene.

thought.background: Optimal TFA equivalent is 4.0. Chloroform gave 85% yield, toluene gave 56%.
thought.gap: Finding an optimal solvent for the reaction.
thought.decision: To test DCM (CH2Cl2) as a solvent with 4.0 equivalents of TFA, expecting similar or better results than chloroform due to its similar polarity.

outcome.observation: The reaction in CH2Cl2 gave a similar yield of 80%.

evidence.text_span: The reaction also proceeded in other solvents: similar yield using CH2Cl2, albeit with lower yield using toluene.

thought.background: Optimized conditions for azepino[4,5-b]indole formation (4.0 equiv TFA in CHCl3 or DCM). Now, apply this to the total synthesis of ngouniensines.
thought.gap: Synthesizing the azepino[4,5-b]indole core of ngouniensines using the developed C-H functionalization.
thought.decision: To couple 2a with rac-3v under the optimized conditions to form rac-4v, which is the azepino[4,5-b]indole core of ngouniensines.

outcome.observation: The first C–H functionalization involving the coupling of 2a and rac-3v successfully built up the azepino[4,5-b]indole core, affording rac-4v in 86% yield with 1:1 dr.

evidence.text_span: The first C–H functionalization involving the coupling of 2a and rac-3v successfully built up the azepino[4,5-b]indole core, affording rac-4v in 86% yield with 1 : 1 dr.

thought.background: The azepino[4,5-b]indole core (rac-4v) has been successfully synthesized. The next step is to introduce the exocyclic conjugated alkene.
thought.gap: Performing the second C-H functionalization to install the exocyclic alkene.
thought.decision: To react rac-4v with formaldehyde under acidic conditions (TFA) in t-BuOH at elevated temperature, expecting the formation of the exocyclic alkene via aldol condensation and retro-Mannich reaction.

outcome.observation: The second C–H functionalization of rac-4v with formaldehyde rapidly installed the exocyclic conjugated alkene, delivering rac-7 upon subsequent amide formation with K2CO3.

evidence.text_span: The second C–H functionalization of rac-4v with formaldehyde rapidly installed the exocyclic conjugated alkene, delivering rac-7 upon subsequent amide formation with K2CO3.

thought.background: The exocyclic alkene has been installed, forming rac-7. The final step is to reduce the amide group to complete the total synthesis of ngouniensines.
thought.gap: Reducing the amide group in rac-7 to obtain the natural products (±)-ngouniensines.
thought.decision: To use LiAlH4 in THF at 0 °C to room temperature for the reduction of the amide group in rac-7.

outcome.observation: Reduction of the amide group produced the natural products (±)-ngouniensines (1a:1b = 1:1) in 66% yield.

evidence.text_span: Finally, reduction of the amide group produced the natural products (±)-ngouniensines (1a : 1b = 1 : 1).

Overall Yield.interpretation: Overall yield not explicitly stated, but individual steps are high yielding.

Longest Linear Steps.interpretation: The synthesis involves 3 linear steps from 2a and rac-3v to (±)-ngouniensines.

Total Steps.interpretation: The total synthesis of ngouniensines is achieved in 3 steps.

Failed Attempts Count.interpretation: Five failed attempts were explicitly reported during the optimization of the first C-H functionalization step.

validation.significance: The work developed an unprecedented C-H functionalization approach for the synthesis of the azepino[4,5-b]indole skeleton, a core structure in many indole alkaloids. This method was successfully applied to the concise total synthesis of ngouniensines, demonstrating the utility of C-H functionalization in simplifying retrosynthetic disconnections and expanding the scope of Pictet-Spengler type reactions. The operational simplicity and broad substrate scope suggest wide applicability for synthesizing azepino[4,5-b]indole containing molecules.

source.title: C-H functionalization of 2-alkyl tryptamines: direct assembly of azepino[4,5-b]indoles and total synthesis of ngouniensines.
source.authors: Xie K, Shen Z, Cheng P, Dong H, Yu ZX, Zu L.
